subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data recording and processing are communicated both clearly and precisely, with wellâlabeled tables, units, error bars and RÂ² values
Uncertainties are systematically considered and propagated through calculations, with error bars correctly displayed
Processing applies Snellâs law correctly with sample calculations and linear regressions clearly shown
weaknesses: Minor formatting inconsistencies in tables (misalignment, wrapped text)
Uncertainty propagation is handled simplistically (linear addition instead of quadrature)
Significant figures are inconsistently applied across data and graphs
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three strands (communication of data processing; consideration of uncertainties) meet the 5â6 descriptor fully, and while the data processing strand has minor omissions, the overall work exhibits exemplary clarity, precision and systematic uncertainty treatment. This justifies a topâband score of 6 for Data Analysis.

strengths: Research question is described within a specific and appropriate optical context
Detailed apparatus list with stated uncertainties and a labeled reproducible diagram
Variables table clearly identifies independent, dependent and controlled factors with rationale
weaknesses: Some methodological steps remain ambiguous (e.g., precise placement of graph paper, definition of âdistance between raysâ)
Uncertainties for a few key apparatus items (e.g., laboratory stand, sugar) are missing
The theoretical linkage (e.g., LorentzâLorenz relation) is oversimplified and lacks cited justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The research question is well articulated and situated in a specific context, satisfying the top band for context. The methodology is described in enough detail to reproduce the work with few ambiguities, placing it in the 3â4 band, but overall the criterion shows strong performance with minor omissions. A holistic score of 5 reflects this mix of highâquality context and mostly clear methodology with room for improvement.

strengths: Conclusion is justified and fully consistent with the processed analysis, referencing numerical gradients and RÂ² values
The research question and hypothesis are restated clearly and linked directly to the findings
weaknesses: Comparison to accepted scientific context is relevant but brief, attributing discrepancies mainly to human error without deeper analysis
No discussion of how identified outliers might affect the overall conclusion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion robustly ties results to the hypothesis and data analysis, fulfilling the topâband descriptor for relevance and support. The scientific context comparison is present but not sufficiently in-depth to warrant the highest mark in that strand, making a score of 5 appropriate for Conclusion.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified and their impact on measurement uncertainty is clearly explained
Realistic and relevant improvements are proposed for each identified limitation
weaknesses: Improvements lack quantitative estimates of their expected impact
No temperature monitoring data are provided to demonstrate control efficacy
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Methodological weaknesses are explained with respect to their impact, satisfying the 5â6 descriptor, while suggested improvements are realistic and relevant but described rather than fully explained with quantitative detail, fitting the 3â4 band for that strand. A balanced, holistic score of 5 reflects these strengths and areas for deeper development.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is clearly focused (percentage sugar concentration vs. refractive index) and the introduction situates it in a specific optical context (applications in the eye, lenses, engineering).  This constitutes a specific and appropriate context rather than a broad or generic one.  Minor digressions (personal interest) do not detract substantially, so the strand fits the 5â6 descriptor; a mid-band 5 is appropriate given a few contextual paragraphs are descriptive rather than analytical.

Instrument uncertainties are quoted; percentage uncertainties are propagated for angles and refractive index; error bars appear on graphs.  Some propagation is simplistic (adds rather than combines in quadrature) and the very large % uncertainties at high concentrations are not discussed, yet overall the treatment is systematic and appropriate.  This aligns with the 5-6 descriptor; mark 5 reflects minor methodological weakness.

Variables are tabulated with rationale; controlled factors (wavelength, incidence angle) and uncontrolled factors (temperature) are identified and methods of control/monitoring stated.  The student describes why each matters but does not fully explain the physics linking every control to data sufficiency, nor justify the chosen range (0â50 %) or number of trials.  Hence the strand is âdescribedâ rather than fully âexplainedâ, fitting band 3-4; score 4.

Three specific weaknesses (solution homogeneity, incidence-angle stability, ambient light) are identified with clear explanations of how each distorts refraction measurements.  The discussion explicitly links each weakness to data scatter or systematic error magnitude, satisfying the requirement to âexplain relative impactâ.  Minor depth gaps justify a 5 rather than 6.

The apparatus list includes uncertainties, a labelled diagram, and a numbered procedure detailing preparation, measurement and repetition for each concentration.  Another student could reproduce the work, although some ambiguities remain (e.g. exact placement of graph paper, definition of the âdistance between raysâ).  This matches the 3-4 band (âfew ambiguitiesâ), so a top-of-band 4 is awarded.

Snellâs law is applied correctly; sample calculations are shown; linear regressions are produced.  However, refractive-index values beyond 1.7 for sugar solutions are unrealistic, indicating either measurement or calculation inaccuracy.  Inconsistencies in significant figures and mixing of maximum/minimum labels persist.  Therefore processing has âsome significant omissions or inaccuraciesâ, placing it securely in band 3-4; a 4 recognises generally competent but imperfect analysis.

For every weakness the student proposes a realistic and relevant improvement (warm water with caveat, fixing stand, dark room), giving some justification.  Explanations are present but not deeply developed (e.g., no quantitative estimate of improvement).  This meets the âdescribedâ criterion of band 3-4; a high-band 4 is awarded.

Data tables carry correct headings, units and uncertainties; processed tables are clearly separated; graphs are labelled with axes, units, trend lines, RÂ² and error bars.  Minor formatting slips (wrapped text, column mis-alignment) prevent perfection but both clarity and precision are largely met, justifying placement in the 5-6 band.  A conservative 5 is given.

The student compares findings with published refractive-index ranges and two external experiments, noting both agreement in trend and discrepancies in magnitude.  The discussion is relevant yet brief; reasons for deviation are attributed mainly to human error without deeper optical analysis.  This fits âsome relevant comparisonâ, band 3-4, so score 4.

The student restates the research question, summarises key graphical results and explicitly links them to the hypothesis.  Conclusions reference numerical gradients and RÂ² values and are internally consistent with processed data.  Minor reservations (no comment on outliersâ influence) lower the certainty, but the conclusion is still âjustified and fully consistentâ, warranting a 5 in the top band.

annotations.comment: The refractiveâindex vs. concentration graph axes are not fully labeled with units for error bars. Label each axis and explain the bar values.
The discussion of trend line and $$R^2$$ is strong; consider adding commentary on identified outliers and their potential causes.
The conclusion restates the hypothesis and links to the data via $$R^2$$ values. To improve, discuss how the few outliers might affect the overall conclusion.
Comparison with published refractiveâindex values is appropriate, but the discussion attributing differences solely to human error lacks critical depth. Analyze potential systematic biases.

The research question is embedded in a specific optical context, linking sugar concentration to refractive index applications. Consider trimming the personal-interest digression to maintain focus on the scientific context.
The introduction is descriptive but lacks a clear connection to the specific sugar-solution investigation. Link the discussion of eye optics more directly to your experimentâs purpose.
Figure 1 lacks a proper source citation and does not specify the refractiveâindex values for each part of the eye. Add a reference and clarify the numerical data in the caption.
The LaTeX equation $$n=\frac{c}{V}$$ is correctly formatted, aiding precise communication of the refractiveâindex definition.
Symbols in the refractiveâindex definition are not all defined (e.g., $$V_{medium}$$). Provide clear definitions for each symbol to ensure reproducibility.
When introducing Snellâs law, note the assumption of monochromatic light and uniform medium properties to clarify experimental constraints.
The link between solution density and refractive index is oversimplified. Cite the LorentzâLorenz relation or another model to justify this physical interpretation.
The hypothesis is concise and directly testable, stating both the independent and dependent relationships between sugar concentration, refraction angle, and refractive index.
The variables table is hard to read due to fragmented text. Redraft the table so each variableâs description, effect, and control method appear in single, coherent rows.
The experimental setup diagram should include a scale or dimensions on each component to allow exact reproduction of geometry.
Some listed apparatus lack stated uncertainties (e.g., laboratory stand, sugar). Include uncertainties for all key instruments for completeness.
The step âdistance between corner bottom of the aquarium and the standâ is unclear. Define exactly how each distance is measured on the diagram.
Rawâdata recording mentions distances measured but omits the number of repetitions or trials. Specify how many measurements per concentration were taken.
Riskâassessment table lists hazards but does not justify the assigned significance level. Explain the basis for each risk ranking.
Include uncertainty values explicitly in raw data table column headings for clear communication of measurement precision.
Uncertainties for volume and mass are summed linearly; consider using quadrature propagation for a more accurate uncertainty treatment.
The sample calculation of $$\theta=\tan^{-1}(x/h)$$ clearly shows how uncertainties affect angle determination.
The propagation of percentage uncertainty in $$\Delta\theta/\theta\times100\%$$ is correctly applied.
Some significant figures are inconsistent (e.g., reporting angles to one decimal in some places and none in others). Standardize to meaningful sig-fig rules.
The processed data table lacks a dedicated column for the calculated refractive index; separate presentation would improve clarity.
The application of $$n=\sin\theta_i/\sin\theta_r$$ is correctly executed, demonstrating appropriate data processing.
Strengths are linked to trigonometric methods and variable control, showing specific positive impacts on uncertainty reduction.
Weaknesses and improvements are realistic and directly address identified limitations, but lack quantitative estimates of their impact. Add approximate effects.
The limitation of temperature is identified but no monitoring data are provided. Include recorded temperature values to quantify control efficacy.

rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
rubricData.name: Physics IA
